name: La Ermita de Virgen de la peña desde Cilleros - circular por la Sierra de Santa Olalla
region_intro: Mountain biking around Cilleros offers diverse terrain within the province of Cáceres, Extremadura, Spain. The region is characterized by mountainous landscapes, including the Sierra de Gata and Sierra de Santa Olalla, providing varied elevation changes. Riders can explore routes that traverse river areas like the Rivera de Gata and lead through natural features. The area provides a network of mountain bike routes suitable for various skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Cilleros The most popular mountain bike trail is PR-CC 187 El Chorrerón Trail, an easy 10.5 miles (16.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 14 minutes to complete. This route offers an engaging ride through the local landscape. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Trevejo desde Hoyos — circular por la Sierra del Gata, a moderate 16.3 miles (26.2 km) path. This circular route explores the terrain of the Sierra de Gata mountains. Local mountain bikers also love the La Ermita de Virgen de la peña desde Cilleros - circular por la Sierra de Santa Olalla, a 14.8 miles (23.9 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Santa Olalla, often completed in about 1 hour 44 minutes. Mountain biking around Cilleros is defined by the Sierra de Gata mountain range, the Sierra de Santa Olalla, and the Rivera de Gata river area. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cilleros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of mountain bike trails can I find around Cilleros?
A: Cilleros offers a diverse range of mountain bike trails, catering to various skill levels. You'll find everything from easy, relatively flat routes to challenging paths with significant elevation changes, particularly within the Sierra de Gata mountain range. In total, there are over 25 mountain bike routes in the area.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Cilleros?
A: Yes, Cilleros has several easy mountain bike trails perfect for beginners or families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022994285/pr-cc-187-el-chorreron-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;PR-CC 187 El Chorrerón Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 10.5-mile (16.8 km) route that offers an engaging ride through the local landscape with minimal elevation gain.

Q: Where can I find challenging mountain bike routes in the Cilleros region?
A: For more challenging mountain biking, head towards the Sierra de Gata. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1252070202/sierra-de-gata-jalama-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Gata: Jálama Loop&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation changes, with over 1500 meters of ascent, providing a demanding experience for advanced riders.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available around Cilleros?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in the Cilleros area are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Notable circular routes include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1250953529/trevejo-desde-hoyos-circular-por-la-sierra-del-gata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trevejo desde Hoyos — circular por la Sierra del Gata&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1252115935/la-ermita-de-virgen-de-la-pena-desde-cilleros-circular-por-la-sierra-de-santa-olalla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Ermita de Virgen de la peña desde Cilleros - circular por la Sierra de Santa Olalla&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while mountain biking near Cilleros?
A: The region boasts beautiful natural scenery. You can explore the rugged terrain of the Sierra de Gata and Sierra de Santa Olalla mountain ranges. Some routes also traverse the Rivera de Gata river area. For specific natural highlights, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2645190" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penha Garcia Natural Swimming Pool and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5995492" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path to the Mirador del Boquerón and Laguna de los Jornus&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical landmarks near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, Cilleros and its surroundings offer cultural points of interest. You might encounter traditional villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6050936" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Martín de Trevejo&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6432832" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoyos&lt;/a&gt;, which provide a glimpse into local culture. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5575765" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parish of Our Lady of the Good Man, Hoyos&lt;/a&gt; is another notable religious building in the area.

Q: What is the typical duration for mountain bike trails in Cilleros?
A: Trail durations vary significantly based on length and difficulty. Easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022994285/pr-cc-187-el-chorreron-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;PR-CC 187 El Chorrerón Trail&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in just over an hour, while moderate trails such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1250667232/el-bano-de-la-cochina-desde-moraleja-circular-por-la-presa-rivera-de-gata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Baño de La Cochina desde Moraleja&lt;/a&gt; might take around 2.5 hours. More difficult routes can extend to several hours, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1252070202/sierra-de-gata-jalama-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Gata: Jálama Loop&lt;/a&gt; which typically takes over 7 hours.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about Cilleros?
A: The komoot community frequently praises the diverse terrain and scenic beauty of the Cilleros region. Riders appreciate the variety of routes, from gentle paths along river areas to challenging climbs in the Sierra de Gata, offering something for every skill level. The natural landscapes and opportunities to explore traditional villages are also highly valued.

Q: Are there any trails that feature water bodies or lakes?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate water features. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1250667232/el-bano-de-la-cochina-desde-moraleja-circular-por-la-presa-rivera-de-gata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Baño de La Cochina desde Moraleja — circular por la Presa Rivera de Gata&lt;/a&gt; route explores the Rivera de Gata river area. You can also find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5578301" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penha Garcia Dam&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5992374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cochina Bath&lt;/a&gt; nearby.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Cilleros?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, Extremadura generally experiences hot summers. Spring and autumn are typically ideal for outdoor activities like mountain biking, offering milder temperatures and pleasant conditions for exploring the region's trails.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that pass through or near traditional villages?
A: Absolutely. The region around Cilleros is dotted with charming traditional villages. Some routes may pass through or near settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6050936" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Martín de Trevejo&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6432832" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoyos&lt;/a&gt;, allowing you to experience the local culture and architecture as part of your ride.
tips: A historic town dating back to Roman times, Cilleros is one of the largest towns in the Sierra de Gata. Located on the fertile agricultural plains of the Alagón River and the Sierras de Villuercas and Gata, the town is a natural gateway to adventures on both the Vía de la Plata and Vía Dalmacia.
Climb the Sierra de Santa Olalla and at 685 masl arrive at the ruins of a small chapel and incredible views of the Cilleros valley landscape below.
